forbiddenClaims: real-time, instant, or guaranteed-latency alert delivery
severity as an authoritative importance score, confidence percentage, or 'will move markets' promise
alert-volume, subscriber, accuracy, or delivery-speed metric not supplied in the brief
AI summary presented as the publisher's headline, an editorial verdict, or a source of record inside a notification or offline view
silent substitution of a machine summary for a publisher article when the source is not downloaded offline
notification controls that cannot be reversed or that bury the material-breakthrough exception
affiliation with any real news publisher, exchange, index provider, rating agency, or device-vendor notification partnership

rubricAnchors.score1: Alerts promise real-time delivery or authoritative severity, or silently swap an AI summary for a publisher article offline.
rubricAnchors.score3: Timing and severity are mostly hedged but one alert reads as a market-impact promise or the offline substitution goes unnoticed.
rubricAnchors.score5: Delivery is an expectation not a guarantee, severity is inferred relevance, AI summaries are labelled and source-attributed, and notification controls are reversible with a material-breakthrough exception.

rubricAnchors.score1: alert-subscription, saved-stories, and notification-controls share no common direction system and disagree on how severity or AI summaries are labelled.
rubricAnchors.score3: Shared tokens across the three screens but the severity label, estimate framing, or AI-summary marker is applied inconsistently.
rubricAnchors.score5: A single direction system unifies all three screens, with a consistent inferred-severity label, a consistent user-derived-estimate framing for volume, and a consistent machine-generated AI-summary marker.

rubricAnchors.score1: No actionable blueprint; engineers cannot infer the alert-subscription, saved-stories, or notification-controls states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Partial blueprints with unresolved volume-preview-visible, source-not-downloaded, or material-breakthrough-exception-on states.
rubricAnchors.score5: Engineers can build all three screens directly, including volume-preview-visible, source-not-downloaded, save-failed-retry, quiet-hours-active, batched-delivery-active, material-breakthrough-exception-on, and all-muted states with their mobile rules.

rubricAnchors.score1: A direct clone of an existing finance-alerts surface that promises real-time delivery or authoritative severity.
rubricAnchors.score3: Derivative of common alert patterns but the inferred-severity and honest-offline treatment is visually distinguishable.
rubricAnchors.score5: A distinct treatment that makes inferred severity, user-derived volume estimates, and honest offline attribution legible without promising speed or authority.

rubricAnchors.score1: No accessibility handling and no source-not-downloaded, save-failed-retry, or all-muted states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Baseline contrast and focus with partial handling of source-not-downloaded or save-failed-retry.
rubricAnchors.score5: WCAG-aligned contrast and focus across all three screens, with source-not-downloaded, save-failed-retry, all-muted, and quiet-hours-active states that keep attribution intact and the material-breakthrough exception reachable.

rubricAnchors.score1: A real-time, guaranteed-latency, authoritative-severity, alert-volume, or accuracy claim appears unsupported, or an AI summary silently substitutes for missing publisher text offline.
rubricAnchors.score3: Most claims trace to the brief but one severity reads as a market-impact promise or one offline item loses its source attribution.
rubricAnchors.score5: Every claim traces to the brief, timing is an expectation, severity is inferred relevance, volume previews are user-derived estimates, and no delivery-speed, accuracy, or authority claim appears.

adjudicationNotes: This is a zero-tolerance case for delivery-speed and severity-authority claims: any real-time, instant, guaranteed-latency, or authoritative-severity framing is an automatic fail on evidence-discipline, regardless of polish.
Offline attribution is load-bearing: an output that silently swaps a machine summary for a publisher article when the source is not downloaded fails product-appropriateness and evidence-discipline.
Notification controls must be reversible and expose the material-breakthrough exception adjacent to quiet hours; an output that buries or omits it fails accessibility-and-failure-states.
Volume previews must read as user-derived estimates, not published benchmarks; an output that frames expected volume as a guaranteed cadence fails evidence-discipline.
Do not prescribe exact copy, brand colours, or pixel-level layout; the brief fixes the timing-not-guaranteed and severity-not-authoritative constraints, not the visual identity.
